questions.question: A recipe for making cookies requires $2 cdot 3$ cups of flour. If the bakery has $15$ cups of flour, how many batches can they make?
A construction company has $5$ workers, and their daily working hours are $8$, $10$, $7$, $9$, $8$. What is the mode of their working hours?
A set of architectural plans has the following dimensions in inches: $10$, $12$, $15$, $11$, $13$. What is the median of these dimensions?
The mean of $5$ test scores is $80$. If one score is $70$, what is the sum of the other $4$ scores?
The median of $3$ numbers is $7$. If the numbers are $5$, $7$, and $x$, what is the value of $x$?
The mode of the following set of numbers is $4$: $2$, $4$, $6$, $4$, $8$. What percentage of the numbers is $4$?
The mean of two numbers is $9$, and their difference is $3$. What are the numbers?
A set of measurements has the following values: $10$, $12$, $14$, $16$, $18$. What is the range of these measurements?

A bakery is making a special batch of bread. The recipe requires $2$ cups of flour per loaf. The bakery has $25$ cups of flour and wants to make as many loaves as possible. Calculate the maximum number of loaves they can make and explain your reasoning.

A construction company is building a new high-rise. The blueprint has the following dimensions: length $= 50$ meters, width $= 20$ meters. Calculate the area of the building and explain how you would use the mean, median, and mode to analyze the dimensions of the building.

teacher_rules: Be sure to check for unit consistency in calculations.
Encourage students to use real-world applications to understand concepts.
Ensure students show all work and explain reasoning for free response questions.
